stemHtml: An aircraft is being vectored for a GLS approach. ATC has given a final heading in order for the aircraft to intercept the final approach course at 25 NM from the landing runway threshold. What should the pilot do?

options.a: Accept the vector because it is within the minimum software-designed coverage area for this type of approach.
options.b: They should NOT accept the vector because it is outside the minimum software-designed coverage area for this approach
options.c: They may accept this vector because satellite-based approaches have NO minimum software-designed coverage area limitations.
options.d: They should NOT accept the vector because the minimum software-designed coverage area for this approach is 12 NM from the landing runway threshold.
